podcasts.name: Lenny's Podcast
podcasts.subtitle: Product | Career | Growth
podcasts.blurb: 面向产品和创业从业者，聚焦增长策略、职业发展与顶尖实践。

episodes.title: From skeptic to true believer: How OpenClaw changed my life | Claire Vo
episodes.description:
Claire Vo is the host of our sister podcast, “How I AI,” a former product executive and engineer, and founder of an AI startup called ChatPRD. Claire now runs her business, podcast, and family life with the help of nine OpenClaw agents running on multiple Mac Minis and old laptops. In this episode, Claire shares her journey from OpenClaw skeptic (it deleted her family calendar the first time she tried it) to true believer, and gives a masterclass in using AI agents in real life.
We discuss:
1. The exact step-by-step process to install and set up OpenClaw (it’s easier than you think)
2. How to avoid the biggest OpenClaw mistakes (don’t install it on your main computer)
3. Actual use cases that have changed Claire’s life (e.g. family scheduling, inbound sales, podcast prep, and course management)
4. Why multiple specialized agents beat one general-purpose agent
5. The security risks everyone worries about—and how to handle them
6. Browser limitations, memory issues, and practical workarounds
episodes.descriptionCN:
Claire Vo 是我们的姊妹播客“How I AI”的主持人，她曾担任产品主管和工程师，也是一家名为 ChatPRD 的人工智能初创公司的创始人。 Claire 现在在多台 Mac Mini 和旧笔记本电脑上运行的 9 个 OpenClaw 代理的帮助下经营她的生意、播客和家庭生活。在这一集中，克莱尔分享了她从 OpenClaw 怀疑论者（她第一次尝试时就删除了她的家庭日历）到真正的信徒的旅程，并提供了在现实生活中使用人工智能代理的大师班。
我们讨论：
1. 安装和设置 OpenClaw 的确切分步过程（比���想象的更容易）
2. 如何避免最大的 OpenClaw 错误（不要将其安装在您的主计算机上）
3. 改变克莱尔生活的实际用例（例如家庭安排、入站销售、播客准备和课程管理）
4. 为什么多个专业代理胜过一个通用代理
5. 每个人都担心的安全风险以及如何应对
6. 浏览器限制、内存问题和实际解决方法

episodes.title: The art of influence: The single most important skill that AI can’t replace | Jessica Fain (Webflow, ex-Slack)
episodes.description:
Jessica Fain is a product leader at Webflow and former Chief of Staff to the CPO at Slack, where she worked alongside April Underwood and many past podcast guests including Stewart Butterfield, Annie Pearl, Tamar Yehoshua, and Noah Weiss. She’s spent her career learning how executives actually make decisions—and why most people completely misunderstand the process.
We discuss:
1. Why great ideas often don’t get buy-in
2. Why executive calendars are “like strobe lights” and why the first 30 seconds of a meeting matter so much
3. Why executives are usually optimizing for a global maximum while you are often optimizing locally
4. The best question Jessica uses when a leader says something that seems wrong: “That’s so interesting. What led you to believe that?”
5. Why you should go in to learn, not to convince
6. Why showing only one option is a mistake
7. Why AI will make influence more important, not less
episodes.descriptionCN:
Jessica Fain 是 Webflow 的产品负责人，也是 Slack 的前首席产品官，她与 April Underwood 以及包括 Stewart Butterfield、Annie Pearl、Tamar Yehoshua 和 Noah Weiss 在内的许多过去的播客嘉宾一起工作。她的职业生涯一直在研究高管们实际上是如何做出决策的，以及为什么大多数人完全误解了这个过程。
我们讨论：
1. 为什么伟大的想法常常得不到认可
2. 为什么高管日历“就像闪光灯”以及为什么会议的前 30 秒如此重要
3. 为什么高管通常会针对全局最大化进行优化，而你则经常进行局部优化
4. 当领导者说出看似错误的事情时，杰西卡使用的最佳问题是：“这太有趣了。是什么让你相信这一点？”
5. 为什么你应该去学习，而不是去说服
6. 为什么只显示一个选项是错误的
7. 为什么人工智能会让影响力变得更加重要，而不是更少

episodes.title: The tactical playbook for getting 20-40% more comp (without sounding greedy) | Jacob Warwick (Executive Negotiator)
episodes.description:
Jacob Warwick is an executive negotiation coach who helps senior operators negotiate better salary, equity, titles, and severance packages. He has worked with leaders across tech and Hollywood, was previously a founder and CEO himself, and has helped clients secure millions in additional compensation. His approach focuses on collaboration over confrontation, understanding motivations, and treating job searches like enterprise sales processes.
We discuss:
1. Why a simple “What’s the chance there’s a little more here?” often unlocks a 20% bump
2. Why Jacob sees 40% average movement when negotiations are run well
3. When negotiation actually starts (hint: it’s much earlier than you think)
4. Why information + timing create power
5. The biggest mistakes people make when negotiating
6. How to navigate the important “What’s your comp expectation?” question without anchoring too low
7. Why the best interviews feel more like discovery calls than interrogations
episodes.descriptionCN:
雅各布·沃里克（Jacob Warwick）是一名高管谈判教练，帮助高级经营者谈判更好的薪资、股权、头衔和遣散费。他曾与科技界和好莱坞的领导者合作，此前曾担任创始人兼首席执行官，并帮助客户获得了数百万美元的额外报酬。他的方法侧重于合作而非对抗、理解动机以及将求职视为企业销售流程。
我们讨论：
1. 为什么是一个简单的“这里还有什么机会？”通常会��锁 20% 的提升
2. 为什么雅各布认为谈判进展顺利时平均变动率为 40%
3. 谈判何时真正开始（提示：比你想象的要早得多）
4. 为什么信息+时机创造力量
5.人们在谈判时犯的最大错误
6. 如何应对重要的“你的薪酬期望是什么？”问题没有锚定太低
7. 为什么最好的采访感觉更像是发现电话而不是审讯

episodes.title: How I built a 1M+ subscriber newsletter and top 10 tech podcast | Lenny Rachitsky
episodes.description:
People have been asking me to sit on the other side of the mic for a long time. With my wife’s debut children’s book, Charts for Babies, coming out next month, we figured: why not do it together? What followed was one of the most honest conversations I’ve had on this podcast. Michelle asked things no one else would think to ask—and many things I’ve never shared publicly. You’ll hear about the specific moments that pushed me to start the newsletter, how I think about quality and iteration, what most stresses me out, and the scariest moment of my life. This was so fun, and so special, and I hope you like it.
We discuss:
1. The collection of moments that led me to what I do now
2. When I added a paywall, and how I knew it was working
3. The hidden treadmill behind shipping a newsletter post and podcast episode every week
4. The most stressful moments I’ve had in business and in life
5. How I think about stress, consistency, and keeping the business small
Pre-order Charts for Babies:
episodes.descriptionCN:
很长一段时间以来，人们一直要求我坐在麦克风的另一边。我妻子的首部儿童读物《婴儿图表》将于下个月出版，我们想：为什么不一起写呢？接下来是我在这个播客上进行过的最诚实的对话之一。米歇尔问了一些其他人不会想问的问题，还有很多我从未公开分享过的问题。您会听到促使我开始撰写时事通讯的具体时刻、我对质量和迭代的看法、给我带来最大压力的事情以及我一生中最可怕的时刻。这太有趣了，也太特别了，我希望你喜欢它。
我们讨论：
1. 引导我做出现在所做的事情的瞬间集合
2. 当我添加付费专区时，我如何知道它正在发挥作用
3. 每周发布时事通讯帖子和播客剧集背后隐藏的跑步机
4. 我在工作和生活中经历过的最有压力的时刻
5. 我如何看待压力、一致性和保持业务规模小
婴儿预购表：

episodes.title: The most successful AI company you’ve never heard of | Qasar Younis
episodes.description:
Qasar Younis is the co-founder and CEO of Applied Intuition, a $15 billion AI company that adds intelligence to cars, tractors, planes, submarines, and other vehicles—essentially, Tesla or Waymo without the hardware. He was previously COO of Y Combinator, started his career as an engineer at GM and Bosch, and was born on a farm in Pakistan.
We discuss:
1. Why the biggest AI revolution will play out in mining, farming, construction, and trucking over the next 5 to 10 years, not in software
2. Why Qasar intentionally stayed under the radar for nearly a decade while building Applied Intuition, and why most founders shouldn’t do that
3. The truth about China’s AI capabilities and why comparisons to American companies are fundamentally flawed
4. The company values that drive Applied Intuition: speed above everything, laugh a lot, half the work is follow-up, never disappoint the customer
5. The biggest lessons from Qasar’s stint as YC’s COO, including that the most successful companies show traction very early
6. How reading old books is the best way to build taste
episodes.descriptionCN:
Qasar Younis 是 Applied Intuition 的联合创始人兼首席执行官，这是一家价值 150 亿美元的人工智能公司，为汽车、拖拉机、飞机、潜艇和其他车辆（本质上是没有硬件的 Tesla 或 Waymo）添加智能。他出生于巴基斯坦的一个农场，曾担任 Y Combinator 的首席运营官，其职业生涯始于通用汽车和博世的工程师。
我们讨论：
1. 为什么最大的人工智能革命将在未来 5 到 10 年内发生在采矿、农业、建筑和卡车运输领域，而不是软件领域
2. 为什么 Qasar 在构建 Applied Intuition 的���程中有意隐藏了近十年的时间，以及为什么大多数创始人不应该这样做
3. 中国人工智能能力的真相以及为何与美国公司的比较存在根本性缺陷
4. 驱动应用直觉的公司价值观：速度高于一切，多笑，一半的工作是跟进，永远不会让客户失望
5. Qasar 担任 YC 首席运营官期间的最大教训，包括最成功的公司很早就表现出了吸引力
6. 阅读旧书如何是培养品味的最佳方式

episodes.title: The design process is dead. Here’s what’s replacing it. | Jenny Wen (head of design at Claude)
episodes.description:
Jenny Wen leads design for Claude at Anthropic. Prior to this, she was Director of Design at Figma, where she led the teams behind FigJam and Slides. Before that, she was a designer at Dropbox, Square, and Shopify.
We discuss:
1. Why the classic discovery → mock → iterate design process is becoming obsolete
2. What a day in the life of a designer at Anthropic looks like, including her AI tool stack
3. Whether AI will eventually surpass humans in taste and judgment
4. Why Jenny left a director role at Figma to return to IC work at Anthropic
5. The three archetypes Jenny is hiring for now
6. Why chatbot interfaces may be more durable than most people expect
episodes.descriptionCN:
Jenny Wen 在 Anthropic 领导 Claude 的设计。在此之前，她曾担任 Figma 的设计总监，领导 FigJam 和 Slides 的团队。在此之前，她曾担任 Dropbox、Square 和 Shopify 的设计师。
我们讨论：
1. 为什么经典的发现→模拟→迭代设计流程正在变得过时
2. Anthropic 设计师的一天是什么样的，包括她的 AI 工具堆栈
3. AI最终是否会在品味和判断力上超越人类
4. 为什么 Jenny 辞去 Figma 的总监职务，回到 Anthropic 从事 IC 工作
5. Jenny 目前正在招聘的三种原型
6. 为什么聊天机器人界面可能比大多数人预期的更耐用

podcasts.name: The AI Daily Brief
podcasts.subtitle: AI News | Strategy | Industry
podcasts.blurb: 每日 AI 动态速览，覆盖模型进展、政策变化与商业影响。

episodes.title: The Ultimate AI Catch-Up Guide
episodes.description:
NLW presents the current AI landscape where capabilities are accelerating, daily adoption among advanced knowledge workers is widespread, and hallucination rates have dropped dramatically. Debunks common myths by showing AI output varies in quality and excels at augmenting research, writing, images, and automation when treated as an iterative coach rather than a replacement for judgment. Offers practical guidance: prioritize context, run rapid iterate-and-verify cycles, experiment with agents and automations, and avoid outsourcing critical decision-making to confidently wrong outputs.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen:
Get it ad free at
Learn more about the show
episodes.descriptionCN:
NLW 展示了当前的人工智能前景，其中能力正在加速，高级知识工作者的日常采用已广泛使用，并且幻觉率已大幅下降。通过展示人工智能输出的质量各不相同，并在被视为迭代教练而不是判断的替代品时，擅长增强研究、写作、图像和自动化，从而揭穿常见的神话。提供实用指导：优先考虑上下文，运行快速迭代和验证周期，尝试代理和自动化，并避免将关键决策外包给自信错误的输出。

episodes.title: The State of AI - Q2 2026
episodes.description:
Report focuses on an AI second moment driven by agentic systems that turn language models into autonomous, workplace-capable agents. Anthropic Claude Code and OpenClaw-style offerings sparked rapid enterprise adoption, major revenue acceleration, and hyperscaler CapEx commitments in the hundreds of billions. Pentagon concerns over Claude usage and widespread market reactions intensified political and regulatory volatility while enterprises shifted from pilots to production and automation use cases.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen:
Get it ad free at
Learn more about the show
episodes.descriptionCN:
报告重点关注由代理系统驱动的人工智能第二阶段，该系统将语言模型转变为具有工作能力的自主代理。 Anthropic Claude Code 和 OpenClaw 式的产品引发了企业的快速采用、收入的大幅增长以及数千亿美元的超大规模资本支出承诺。五角大楼对克劳德的使用和广泛的市场反应的担忧加剧了政治和监管波动，同时企业从试点转向生产和自动化用例。

episodes.title: The Era of Vertical AI Models
episodes.description:
Analysis contrasts Sutton's Bitter Lesson with a rising era of vertical AI models trained on last-mile interaction data, exemplified by Intercom's Apex and Cursor's Composer Two. Post-training on proprietary interaction datasets and reinforcement learning on curated quality data can elevate open-weight base models to meet or exceed frontier-model performance for specific tasks. Resulting effects include model speciation, a shift to in-house fine-tuning on open models, erosion of API-based moats, and a renewed premium on proprietary evaluation data.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen:
Get it ad free at
Learn more about the show
episodes.descriptionCN:
分析将萨顿的惨痛教训与基于最后一英里交互数据训练的垂直人工智能模型的兴起时代进行了对比，例如 Intercom 的 Apex 和 Cursor 的 Composer Two。对专有交互数据集的后训练和对精选质量数据的强化学习可以提升开放权重基础模型，以满足或超过特定任务的前沿模型性能。由此产生的影响包括模型形成、开放模型向内部微调的转变、基于 API 的护城河的侵蚀以及对专有评估数据的重新重视。
AI 每日简报可帮助您了解 AI 领域最重要的新闻和讨论。

episodes.title: Anthropic's New Mythos Model a "Step Change" in Capabilities
episodes.description:
A leaked draft reveals Anthropic's Claude Mythos, a compute‑intensive, more capable model now in early access and prompting concerns about cybersecurity and release safety. Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live brings continuous, low‑latency conversational voice with improved multi‑step function calling for real‑time agent actions. Shopify's Tinker app bundles 100+ AI commerce tools to simplify branding and product creation for small businesses, while OpenAI upgrades Codex with plugins and shelves an adult mode amid safety and business tradeoffs.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen:
Get it ad free at
Learn more about the show
episodes.descriptionCN:
一份泄露的草案揭示了 Anthropic 的 Claude Mythos，这是一个计算密集型、功能更强大的模型，目前处于抢先体验阶段，引发了人们对网络安全和发布安全的担忧。 Google 的 Gemini 3.1 Flash Live 带来了连续、低延迟的对话语音，以及改进的多步骤功能，可调用实时代理操作。 Shopify 的 Tinker 应用程序捆绑了 100 多个 AI 商务工具，以简化小型企业的品牌推广和产品创建，而 OpenAI 通过插件升级 Codex，并在安全和业务权衡中上架成人模式。

episodes.title: Why AI Needs Better Benchmarks
episodes.description:
ARC-AGI-3 from the ARC Prize measures intelligence by testing learning efficiency across 135 interactive visual games. Coverage examines benchmark saturation, benchmark maxing, and the shift from memorization-focused tests toward interactive, tool-enabled real-world evaluations. ARC-AGI-3 aims to expose gaps between human learning and current models and to refocus research on genuine general intelligence.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen:
Get it ad free at
Learn more about the show
episodes.descriptionCN:
ARC 奖的 ARC-AGI-3 通过测试 135 个交互式视觉游戏的学习效率来衡量智力。覆盖范围检查基准饱和度、基准最大化以及从以记忆为中心的测试向交互式、支持工具的现实世界评估的转变。 ARC-AGI-3 旨在揭示人类学习与当前模型之间的差距，并将研究重新集中在真正的通用智能上。
AI 每日简报可帮助您了解 AI 领域最重要的新闻和讨论。

episodes.title: Work AGI is the Only AGI that Matters
episodes.description:
OpenAI pivots compute and engineering from the consumer video app Sora toward coding and knowledge-work models like Codex and the pretrained Spud. Executive reshuffle trims Sam Altman's direct oversight while folding safety into research and security into scaling under Mark Chen and Greg Brockman. Industry focus shifts from AGI hype to the real work of deploying model-driven automation across complex enterprise tasks.
The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
Subscribe to the podcast version of The AI Daily Brief wherever you listen:
Get it ad free at
Learn more about the show
episodes.descriptionCN:
OpenAI 将计算和工程从消费者视频应用 Sora 转向 Codex 和预训练 Spud 等编码和知识工作模型。高管改组削减了萨姆·奥尔特曼 (Sam Altman) 的直接监督权，同时将安全纳入研究，将安全纳入马克·陈 (Mark Chen) 和格雷格·布罗克曼 (Greg Brockman) 的规模扩张。行业焦点从 AGI 炒作转向跨复杂企业任务部署模型驱动自动化的实际工作。
